steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses 'despite' to set up a concession: even though something was true about the scholar's relationship to her general expertise, she could still speak authoritatively about one specific work. 'Despite' signals that the blank must describe doubt or reluctance — not confidence — toward that broader expertise.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — affinity for: means she liked or felt drawn to her general expertise. 'Despite' an affinity would create no logical concession — a positive feeling doesn't need to be overcome. ✗
  • Choice B — exceptions to: implies she knew of exceptions in her field, which does not explain why she would need to overcome anything to speak about one text. ✗
  • Choice C — hesitancy about: means she felt uncertain or unsure regarding her general expertise. 'Despite' that hesitancy, she still spoke with authority on the specific work — a clean logical concession. ✓
  • Choice D — insight into: means she understood her expertise well. 'Despite' insight also creates no concession — insight would support, not hinder, speaking authoritatively. ✗
That matches Choice C — hesitancy about.
